data.body:
Observed in worktree-20260409-084011 (sprint for epic 6cd2-da08):

merge-to-main.sh completed phases sync, merge, version_bump, and validate (state file confirmed all 4 complete, retry_count=10). However push phase was never recorded as complete.

Result: origin/main = 89dfa6d8 (version 1.11.15, the raw merge commit), local main = 99c6d8f9 (version 1.11.16, the version-bumped commit). Both commits have identical parents (12408211, 6de6c1ea) — they are siblings, not parent-child. A normal git push to origin fails because 99c6d8f9 is not a descendant of 89dfa6d8.

The version_bump phase appears to have created a new parallel merge commit from the same two parents rather than creating a regular commit on top of the existing merge. This caused the divergence.

All feature content (16 files, 1884 insertions) is present on origin/main. The only difference is the version number (1.11.15 vs 1.11.16).

Manually resolved by accepting current origin/main state (1.11.15) and not force-pushing. The version bump was left unpushed.
